preview_text:
The model favors Germany at 60%, with Ivory Coast a clear underdog at 13%. That 47-percentage-point gap reflects a substantial disparity in forecast strength, though the underdog retains a path to victory in roughly one in eight scenarios.

The spread reveals a favorite comfortable but not dominant. The draw sits at 27%, making it the second-most-likely outcome and a meaningful possibility—neither side is projected to overwhelm the other. The match profile suggests Germany as probable winners, but with genuine competitive range; Ivory Coast enters with a realistic chance to disrupt.

The model and sportsbooks diverge modestly. Books price Germany 3 points higher and compress the draw to 21%; Polymarket sits between, slightly favoring the German win further. The gaps are not wide enough to flag a structural disagreement, though the model's higher draw probability (27% vs. 21% at the consensus) merits noting. The dashboard does not adjudicate which reading of the draw likelihood is correct.
